title: Best VPN for Torrenting: What to Look For in 2026
description: Torrenting without a VPN exposes your IP to trackers and your ISP. Here is what actually matters when choosing a VPN for P2P downloads.
content:
Why VPN Matters for Torrenting

When you use BitTorrent, your IP address is visible to every peer in the swarm. Copyright trolls and monitoring organizations collect these IPs and send notices to ISPs. A VPN routes your torrent traffic through an encrypted tunnel, so the IP visible in the swarm belongs to the VPN server, not to you. Your ISP sees encrypted VPN traffic, not torrent traffic. This works as long as the VPN connection stays stable, which is why a kill switch is mandatory for serious torrenting use.

What to Look for in a Torrenting VPN

Four criteria matter. First, P2P-friendly servers: some VPN providers block or throttle torrents on their servers. Look for providers that explicitly allow P2P and designate servers for it. Second, no-logs policy with an audit: the VPN provider must not log connection data or IP addresses. Look for providers with independently audited no-logs policies. Third, kill switch: if the VPN drops, the kill switch must cut all internet access to prevent IP leakage. Test it. Fourth, speed: torrenting benefits from high throughput. WireGuard protocol generally provides the best speed on modern hardware.

Recommended Providers

Mullvad is the strongest choice for privacy-focused users: accepts cash and cryptocurrency, does not require an email address to sign up, audited no-logs policy, WireGuard support, and explicit P2P support. IVPN is a close alternative with similar privacy credentials. NordVPN and ExpressVPN are popular and allow P2P on dedicated servers, but their privacy practices have come under more scrutiny than Mullvad's. Avoid free VPNs for torrenting.

DNS Leak Testing

After connecting to your VPN, go to dnsleaktest.com and run an extended test. If any DNS requests resolve through your real ISP rather than the VPN's DNS servers, you have a DNS leak. Fix this by enabling the VPN's built-in DNS leak protection or by manually setting DNS to the VPN provider's servers.
